title: extractResponseText() uses wrong property name for ThinkingContent — thinking block extraction was broken since implementation
body: extractResponseText() checked 'text' in part for thinking blocks, but pi-ai ThinkingContent has { type: 'thinking', thinking: string }, not { type: 'thinking', text: string }. The fallback to thinking content never fired. Test fixtures used { type: 'thinking', text: '...' } — a made-up shape that matched the code's assumption, not the real type. Tests passed against wrong fixtures. Fixed in fd02c15 — property corrected to 'thinking', test fixtures corrected. Root cause: implementing agent was not scoped with the exact ThinkingContent type signature.
resolved_by: fd02c15 — corrected property access from .text to .thinking, fixed test fixtures to use real ThinkingContent shape
